mnemonics: KHA-LA-SO: Karush-Kuhn-Tucker Conditions, Lagrange Multipliers, Simplex Method - for remembering the three foundational methods in advanced optimization.
keyPoints: The Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions are necessary for a solution in nonlinear programming to be optimal, provided some regularity conditions are satisfied.
The Method of Lagrange Multipliers is a strategy for finding the local maxima and minima of a function subject to equality constraints.

The Simplex Method is an algorithm for solving linear programming problems by constructing a feasible solution at a vertex of the polytope and then walking along edges of the polytope to vertices with improved objective function values.
commonMistakes: Confusing the applicability of KKT conditions (for inequality constraints) with Lagrange Multipliers (for equality constraints only).
Forgetting to check the regularity conditions (like constraint qualification) before applying KKT conditions, which can lead to an incorrect conclusion.
Making arithmetic errors during the pivoting steps of the Simplex Method, which can derail the entire solution process.
